column4: The phrase "um ... zu" is a subordinating conjunction construction used to introduce an infinitive clause that expresses purpose or intention.   Key Rules:    The subject of the main clause and the infinitive clause must be the same.   If the subjects are different, use "damit" instead.   "Um" begins the clause, and "zu" comes immediately before the infinitive verb at the end of the clause.   It is a subordinating construction, so the verb is placed at the end of the clause.   Note: "Um" by itself cannot be used as a conjunction; it only functions together with "zu" in this construction.
